fields.name: HSION
fields.description: HSI16 clock enable Set and cleared by software. Cleared by hardware to stop the HSI16 oscillator when entering Stop, Standby or Shutdown mode. Set by hardware to force the HSI16 oscillator ON when STOPWUCK=1 or HSIASFS = 1 when leaving Stop modes, or in case of failure of the HSE crystal oscillator. This bit is set by hardware if the HSI16 is used directly or indirectly as system clock.

fields.name: HSIKERON
fields.description: HSI16 always enable for peripheral kernels. Set and cleared by software to force HSI16 ON even in Stop modes. The HSI16 can only feed USARTs and I<sup>2</sup>Cs peripherals configured with HSI16 as kernel clock. Keeping the HSI16 ON in Stop mode allows to avoid slowing down the communication speed because of the HSI16 startup time. This bit has no effect on HSION value.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: HSI16 oscillator is forced ON even in Stop mode.

fields.name: HSIRDY
fields.description: HSI16 clock ready flag Set by hardware to indicate that HSI16 oscillator is stable. This bit is set only when HSI16 is enabled by software by setting HSION. Note: Once the HSION bit is cleared, HSIRDY goes low after 6 HSI16 clock cycles.

fields.name: HSEON
fields.description: HSE clock enable Set and cleared by software. Cleared by hardware to stop the HSE oscillator when entering Stop, Standby or Shutdown mode. This bit cannot be reset if the HSE oscillator is used directly or indirectly as the system clock.

fields.name: HSERDY
fields.description: HSE clock ready flag Set by hardware to indicate that the HSE oscillator is stable. Note: Once the HSEON bit is cleared, HSERDY goes low after 6 HSE clock cycles.

fields.name: HSEBYP
fields.description: HSE crystal oscillator bypass Set and cleared by software to bypass the oscillator with an external clock. The external clock must be enabled with the HSEON bit set, to be used by the device. The HSEBYP bit can be written only if the HSE oscillator is disabled.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: HSE crystal oscillator bypassed with external clock

fields.name: CSSON
fields.description: Clock security system enable Set by software to enable the clock security system. When CSSON is set, the clock detector is enabled by hardware when the HSE oscillator is ready, and disabled by hardware if a HSE clock failure is detected. This bit is set only and is cleared by reset.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Clock security system OFF (clock detector OFF)

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Clock security system ON (Clock detector ON if the HSE oscillator is stable, OFF if not).

fields.name: PLLON
fields.description: Main PLL enable Set and cleared by software to enable the main PLL. Cleared by hardware when entering Stop, Standby or Shutdown mode. This bit cannot be reset if the PLL clock is used as the system clock.

fields.name: PLLRDY
fields.description: Main PLL clock ready flag Set by hardware to indicate that the main PLL is locked.

fields.name: HSICAL
fields.description: HSI16 clock calibration These bits are initialized at startup with the factory-programmed HSI16 calibration trim value. When HSITRIM is written, HSICAL is updated with the sum of HSITRIM and the factory trim value.

fields.name: HSITRIM
fields.description: HSI16 clock trimming These bits provide an additional user-programmable trimming value that is added to the HSICAL[7:0] bits. It can be programmed to adjust to variations in voltage and temperature that influence the frequency of the HSI16. The default value is 16, which, when added to the HSICAL value, should trim the HSI16 to 16 MHz 1 %.

fields.name: SWS
fields.description: System clock switch status (set by hardware)

fields.name: MCOPRE
fields.description: Microcontroller clock output prescaler These bits are set and cleared by software. It is highly recommended to change this prescaler before MCO output is enabled. Others: not allowed

enum.name: HSI16
enum.description: HSI16 clock selected as PLL clock entry

enum.name: HSE
enum.description: HSE clock selected as PLL clock entry

fields.name: PLLM
fields.description: Division factor for the main PLL input clock Set and cleared by software to divide the PLL input clock before the VCO. These bits can be written only when all PLLs are disabled. VCO input frequency = PLL input clock frequency / PLLM with 1 <= PLLM <= 16 ... Note: The software has to set these bits correctly to ensure that the VCO input frequency is within the range defined in the device datasheet.

fields.name: PLLN
fields.description: Main PLL multiplication factor for VCO Set and cleared by software to control the multiplication factor of the VCO. These bits can be written only when the PLL is disabled. VCO output frequency = VCO input frequency x PLLN with 8 =< PLLN =< 127 ... ... Note: The software has to set correctly these bits to assure that the VCO output frequency is within the range defined in the device datasheet.

fields.name: PLLPEN
fields.description: Main PLL PLL P clock output enable Set and reset by software to enable the PLL P clock output of the PLL. In order to save power, when the PLL P clock output of the PLL is not used, the value of PLLPEN should be 0.

fields.name: PLLP
fields.description: Main PLL division factor for PLL P clock. Set and cleared by software to control the frequency of the main PLL output clock PLL P clock. These bits can be written only if PLL is disabled. When the PLLPDIV[4:0] is set to 00000PLL P output clock frequency = VCO frequency / PLLP with PLLP =7, or 17 Note: The software has to set these bits correctly not to exceed 170 MHz on this domain.

fields.name: PLLQEN
fields.description: Main PLL Q clock output enable Set and reset by software to enable the PLL Q clock output of the PLL. In order to save power, when the PLL Q clock output of the PLL is not used, the value of PLLQEN should be 0.

fields.name: PLLQ
fields.description: Main PLL division factor for PLL Q clock. Set and cleared by software to control the frequency of the main PLL output clock PLL Q clock. This output can be selected for USB, RNG, SAI (48 MHz clock). These bits can be written only if PLL is disabled. PLL Q output clock frequency = VCO frequency / PLLQ with PLLQ = 2, 4, 6, or 8 Note: The software has to set these bits correctly not to exceed 170 MHz on this domain.

fields.name: PLLREN
fields.description: PLL R clock output enable Set and reset by software to enable the PLL R clock output of the PLL (used as system clock). This bit cannot be written when PLL R clock output of the PLL is used as System Clock. In order to save power, when the PLL R clock output of the PLL is not used, the value of PLLREN should be 0.

fields.name: PLLR
fields.description: Main PLL division factor for PLL R clock (system clock) Set and cleared by software to control the frequency of the main PLL output clock PLLCLK. This output can be selected as system clock. These bits can be written only if PLL is disabled. PLL R output clock frequency = VCO frequency / PLLR with PLLR = 2, 4, 6, or 8 Note: The software has to set these bits correctly not to exceed 170 MHz on this domain.

fields.name: PLLPDIV
fields.description: Main PLLP division factor Set and cleared by software to control the PLL P frequency. PLL P output clock frequency = VCO frequency / PLLPDIV. ....

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: PLL P clock is controlled by the bit PLLP

enum.description: PLL Q clock selected as SAI clock

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: Clock provided on I2S_CKIN pin selected as SAI clock

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: HSI16 clock selected as SAI clock

fields.name: I2S23SEL
fields.description: clock source selection These bits are set and cleared by software to select the I2S23 clock source.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: System clock selected as I2S23 clock

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: PLL Q clock selected as I2S23 clock

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: Clock provided on I2S_CKIN pin is selected as I2S23 clock

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: HSI16 clock selected as I2S23 clock.

fields.name: CLK48SEL
fields.description: 48 MHz clock source selection These bits are set and cleared by software to select the 48 MHz clock source used by USB device FS and RNG.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: HSI48 clock selected as 48 MHz clock

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: PLL Q clock (PLL48M1CLK) selected as 48 MHz clock

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: Reserved, must be kept at reset value

fields.name: ADC12SEL
fields.description: ADC1/2 clock source selection These bits are set and cleared by software to select the clock source used by the ADC interface.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: PLL P clock selected as ADC1/2 clock

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: System clock selected as ADC1/2 clock

fields.name: ADC345SEL
fields.description: ADC3/4/5 clock source selection These bits are set and cleared by software to select the clock source used by the ADC345 interface.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: PLL P clock selected as ADC345 clock

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: System clock selected as ADC3/4/5 clock

fields.name: LSEON
fields.description: LSE oscillator enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: LSERDY
fields.description: LSE oscillator ready Set and cleared by hardware to indicate when the external 32 kHz oscillator is stable. After the LSEON bit is cleared, LSERDY goes low after 6 external low-speed oscillator clock cycles.

fields.name: LSEBYP
fields.description: LSE oscillator bypass Set and cleared by software to bypass oscillator in debug mode. This bit can be written only when the external 32 kHz oscillator is disabled (LSEON=0 and LSERDY=0).

fields.name: LSEDRV
fields.description: LSE oscillator drive capability Set by software to modulate the LSE oscillators drive capability. The oscillator is in Xtal mode when it is not in bypass mode.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Xtal mode medium low driving capability

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: Xtal mode medium high driving capability

fields.name: LSECSSON
fields.description: CSS on LSE enable Set by software to enable the Clock Security System on LSE (32 kHz oscillator). LSECSSON must be enabled after the LSE oscillator is enabled (LSEON bit enabled) and ready (LSERDY flag set by hardware), and after the RTCSEL bit is selected. Once enabled this bit cannot be disabled, except after a LSE failure detection (LSECSSD =1). In that case the software MUST disable the LSECSSON bit.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: CSS on LSE (32 kHz external oscillator) OFF

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: CSS on LSE (32 kHz external oscillator) ON

fields.name: LSECSSD
fields.description: CSS on LSE failure Detection Set by hardware to indicate when a failure has been detected by the Clock Security System on the external 32 kHz oscillator (LSE).

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: No failure detected on LSE (32 kHz oscillator)

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Failure detected on LSE (32 kHz oscillator)

fields.name: RTCSEL
fields.description: RTC clock source selection Set by software to select the clock source for the RTC. Once the RTC clock source has been selected, it cannot be changed anymore unless the RTC domain is reset, or unless a failure is detected on LSE (LSECSSD is set). The BDRST bit can be used to reset them.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: LSE oscillator clock used as RTC clock

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: LSI oscillator clock used as RTC clock

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: HSE oscillator clock divided by 32 used as RTC clock

fields.name: RTCEN
fields.description: RTC clock enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: BDRST
fields.description: RTC domain software reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: LSCOEN
fields.description: Low speed clock output enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: LSCOSEL
fields.description: Low speed clock output selection Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: LSION
fields.description: LSI oscillator enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: LSIRDY
fields.description: LSI oscillator ready Set and cleared by hardware to indicate when the LSI oscillator is stable. After the LSION bit is cleared, LSIRDY goes low after 3 LSI oscillator clock cycles. This bit can be set even if LSION = 0 if the LSI is requested by the Clock Security System on LSE, by the Independent Watchdog or by the RTC.

fields.name: RMVF
fields.description: Remove reset flag Set by software to clear the reset flags.

fields.name: OBLRSTF
fields.description: Option byte loader reset flag Set by hardware when a reset from the Option Byte loading occurs. Cleared by writing to the RMVF bit.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: No reset from Option Byte loading occurred

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Reset from Option Byte loading occurred

fields.name: PINRSTF
fields.description: Pin reset flag Set by hardware when a reset from the NRST pin occurs. Cleared by writing to the RMVF bit.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: No reset from NRST pin occurred

fields.name: BORRSTF
fields.description: BOR flag Set by hardware when a BOR occurs. Cleared by writing to the RMVF bit.

fields.name: SFTRSTF
fields.description: Software reset flag Set by hardware when a software reset occurs. Cleared by writing to the RMVF bit.

fields.name: IWDGRSTF
fields.description: Independent window watchdog reset flag Set by hardware when an independent watchdog reset domain occurs. Cleared by writing to the RMVF bit.

fields.name: WWDGRSTF
fields.description: Window watchdog reset flag Set by hardware when a window watchdog reset occurs. Cleared by writing to the RMVF bit.

fields.name: LPWRRSTF
fields.description: Low-power reset flag Set by hardware when a reset occurs due to illegal Stop, Standby or Shutdown mode entry. Cleared by writing to the RMVF bit.

fields.name: HSI48ON
fields.description: HSI48 clock enable Set and cleared by software. Cleared by hardware to stop the HSI48 when entering in Stop, Standby or Shutdown modes.

fields.name: HSI48RDY
fields.description: HSI48 clock ready flag Set by hardware to indicate that HSI48 oscillator is stable. This bit is set only when HSI48 is enabled by software by setting HSI48ON.

fields.name: HSI48CAL
fields.description: HSI48 clock calibration These bits are initialized at startup with the factory-programmed HSI48 calibration trim value. They are ready only.

fields.name: I2C4SEL
fields.description: I2C4 clock source selection These bits are set and cleared by software to select the I2C4 clock source.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: System clock (SYSCLK) selected as I2C4 clock

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: HSI16 clock selected as I2C4 clock

fields.name: QSPISEL
fields.description: QUADSPI clock source selection Set and reset by software.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: system clock selected as QUADSPI kernel clock

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: HSI16 clock selected as QUADSPI kernel clock

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: PLL Q clock selected as QUADSPI kernel clock

registers.name: C0CR
registers.description: DMAMux - DMA request line multiplexer channel x control register

fields.name: DMAREQ_ID
fields.description: DMA request line selected to route to the DMA channel (see RM0440 Table: DMAMUX request line mapping)

fields.name: SOIE
fields.description: Interrupt enable at synchronization event overrun

fields.name: NBREQ
fields.description: Number of DMA requests to forward Defines the number of DMA requests forwarded before output event is generated. In synchronous mode, it also defines the number of DMA requests to forward after a synchronization event, then stop forwarding. The actual number of DMA requests forwarded is NBREQ+1. Note: This field can only be written when both SE and EGE bits are reset.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: UEV enabled. The Update (UEV) event is generated by one of the following events:

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: UEV disabled. The Update event is not generated, shadow registers keep their value (ARR, PSC, CCRx). However the counter and the prescaler are reinitialized if the UG bit is set or if a hardware reset is received from the slave mode controller.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Any of the following events generate an update interrupt or DMA request if enabled. These events can be:

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Only counter overflow/underflow generates an update interrupt or DMA request if enabled.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Counter is not stopped at update event

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Counter stops counting at the next update event (clearing the bit CEN)

fields.name: CKD
fields.description: Clock division (sampling clock for filters / dead-time)

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: No remapping. UIF status bit is not copied to TIMx_CNT register bit 31.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Remapping enabled. UIF status bit is copied to TIMx_CNT register bit 31.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: CCxE, CCxNE and OCxM bits are not preloaded

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: CCxE, CCxNE and OCxM bits are preloaded, after having been written, they are updated only when a commutation event (COM) occurs (COMG bit set or rising edge detected on tim_trgi, depending on the CCUS bit).

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: When capture/compare control bits are preloaded (CCPC=1), they are updated by setting the COMG bit only

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: When capture/compare control bits are preloaded (CCPC=1), they are updated by setting the COMG bit or when an rising edge occurs on tim_trgi

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: CCx DMA request sent when CCx event occurs

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: CCx DMA requests sent when update event occurs

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Reset - the UG bit from the TIMx_EGR register is used as trigger output (tim_trgo). If the reset is generated by the trigger input (slave mode controller configured in reset mode) then the signal on tim_trgo is delayed compared to the actual reset.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Enable - the Counter Enable signal CNT_EN is used as trigger output (tim_trgo). It is useful to start several timers at the same time or to control a window in which a slave timer is enable. The Counter Enable signal is generated by a logic AND between CEN control bit and the trigger input when configured in gated mode. When the Counter Enable signal is controlled by the trigger input, there is a delay on tim_trgo, except if the master/slave mode is selected (see the MSM bit description in TIMx_SMCR register).

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: Update - The update event is selected as trigger output (tim_trgo). For instance a master timer can then be used as a prescaler for a slave timer.

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: Compare Pulse - The trigger output send a positive pulse when the CC1IF flag is to be set (even if it was already high), as soon as a capture or a compare match occurred. (tim_trgo).

enum.name: B_0x4
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc1refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo)

enum.name: B_0x5
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc2refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo)

enum.name: B_0x6
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc3refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo)

enum.name: B_0x7
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc4refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo)

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: The tim_ti1_in[15:0] multiplexer output is connected to tim_ti1 input

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: tim_ti1_in[15:0], tim_ti2_in[15:0] and tim_ti3_in[15:0] multiplexers outputs are XORed and connected to the tim_ti1 input

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: tim_oc1n=0 after a dead-time when MOE=0

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: tim_oc1n=1 after a dead-time when MOE=0

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Reset - the UG bit from the TIMx_EGR register is used as trigger output (tim_trgo2). If the reset is generated by the trigger input (slave mode controller configured in reset mode), the signal on tim_trgo2 is delayed compared to the actual reset.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Enable - the Counter Enable signal CNT_EN is used as trigger output (tim_trgo2). It is useful to start several timers at the same time or to control a window in which a slave timer is enabled. The Counter Enable signal is generated by a logic AND between the CEN control bit and the trigger input when configured in Gated mode. When the Counter Enable signal is controlled by the trigger input, there is a delay on tim_trgo2, except if the Master/Slave mode is selected (see the MSM bit description in TIMx_SMCR register).

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: Update - the update event is selected as trigger output (tim_trgo2). For instance, a master timer can then be used as a prescaler for a slave timer.

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: Compare pulse - the trigger output sends a positive pulse when the CC1IF flag is to be set (even if it was already high), as soon as a capture or compare match occurs (tim_trgo2).

enum.name: B_0x4
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc1refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo2)

enum.name: B_0x5
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc2refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo2)

enum.name: B_0x6
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc3refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo2)

enum.name: B_0x7
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc4refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo2)

enum.name: B_0x8
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc5refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo2)

enum.name: B_0x9
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc6refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo2)

enum.name: B_0xA
enum.description: Compare Pulse - tim_oc4refc rising or falling edges generate pulses on tim_trgo2

enum.name: B_0xB
enum.description: Compare pulse - tim_oc6refc rising or falling edges generate pulses on tim_trgo2

enum.name: B_0xC
enum.description: Compare pulse - tim_oc4refc or tim_oc6refc rising edges generate pulses on tim_trgo2

enum.name: B_0xD
enum.description: Compare pulse - tim_oc4refc rising or tim_oc6refc falling edges generate pulses on tim_trgo2

enum.name: B_0xE
enum.description: Compare pulse - tim_oc5refc or tim_oc6refc rising edges generate pulses on tim_trgo2

enum.name: B_0xF
enum.description: Compare pulse - tim_oc5refc rising or tim_oc6refc falling edges generate pulses on tim_trgo2

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Slave mode disabled - if CEN = 1' then the prescaler is clocked directly by the internal clock.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Quadrature encoder mode 1, x2 mode- Counter counts up/down on tim_ti1fp1 edge depending on tim_ti2fp2 level.

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: Quadrature encoder mode 2, x2 mode - Counter counts up/down on tim_ti2fp2 edge depending on tim_ti1fp1 level.

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: Quadrature encoder mode 3, x4 mode - Counter counts up/down on both tim_ti1fp1 and tim_ti2fp2 edges depending on the level of the other input.

enum.name: B_0x4
enum.description: Reset Mode - Rising edge of the selected trigger input (tim_trgi) reinitializes the counter and generates an update of the registers.

enum.name: B_0x5
enum.description: Gated Mode - The counter clock is enabled when the trigger input (tim_trgi) is high. The counter stops (but is not reset) as soon as the trigger becomes low. Both start and stop of the counter are controlled.

enum.name: B_0x6
enum.description: Trigger Mode - The counter starts at a rising edge of the trigger tim_trgi (but it is not reset). Only the start of the counter is controlled.

enum.name: B_0x7
enum.description: External Clock Mode 1 - Rising edges of the selected trigger (tim_trgi) clock the counter.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: tim_ocref_clr_int is connected to the tim_ocref_clr input

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: The effect of an event on the trigger input (tim_trgi) is delayed to allow a perfect synchronization between the current timer and its slaves (through tim_trgo). It is useful if we want to synchronize several timers on a single external event.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: No filter, sampling is done at f<sub>DTS</sub>

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: External clock mode 2 enabled. The counter is clocked by any active edge on the tim_etrf signal.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: tim_etr_in is non-inverted, active at high level or rising edge.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: tim_etr_in is inverted, active at low level or falling edge.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: The transfer is triggered by the Timer's Update event

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: The transfer is triggered by the Index event

enum.name: B_0x1

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Reinitialize the counter and generates an update of the registers. Note that the prescaler counter is cleared too (anyway the prescaler ratio is not affected). The counter is cleared if the center-aligned mode is selected or if DIR=0 (upcounting), else it takes the auto-reload value (TIMx_ARR) if DIR=1 (downcounting).

enum.name: B_0x1

enum.description: UEV enabled. The Update (UEV) event is generated by one of the following events:

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: UEV disabled. The Update event is not generated, shadow registers keep their value (ARR, PSC, CCRx). However the counter and the prescaler are reinitialized if the UG bit is set or if a hardware reset is received from the slave mode controller.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Any of the following events generate an update interrupt or DMA request if enabled. These events can be:

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Only counter overflow/underflow generates an update interrupt or DMA request if enabled.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Counter is not stopped at update event

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Counter stops counting at the next update event (clearing the bit CEN)

fields.name: CKD
fields.description: Clock division (sampling clock for filters / dead-time)

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: No remapping. UIF status bit is not copied to TIMx_CNT register bit 31.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Remapping enabled. UIF status bit is copied to TIMx_CNT register bit 31.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: CCx DMA request sent when CCx event occurs

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: CCx DMA requests sent when update event occurs

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Reset - the UG bit from the TIMx_EGR register is used as trigger output (tim_trgo). If the reset is generated by the trigger input (slave mode controller configured in reset mode) then the signal on tim_trgo is delayed compared to the actual reset.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Enable - the Counter enable signal, CNT_EN, is used as trigger output (tim_trgo). It is useful to start several timers at the same time or to control a window in which a slave timer is enabled. The Counter Enable signal is generated by a logic AND between CEN control bit and the trigger input when configured in gated mode.

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: Update - The update event is selected as trigger output (tim_trgo). For instance a master timer can then be used as a prescaler for a slave timer.

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: Compare Pulse - The trigger output send a positive pulse when the CC1IF flag is to be set (even if it was already high), as soon as a capture or a compare match occurred (tim_trgo).

enum.name: B_0x4
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc1refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo)

enum.name: B_0x5
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc2refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo)

enum.name: B_0x6
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc3refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo)

enum.name: B_0x7
enum.description: Compare - tim_oc4refc signal is used as trigger output (tim_trgo)

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: The tim_ti1_in[15:0] multiplexer output is to tim_ti1 input

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: The tim_ti1_in[15:0], tim_ti2_in[15:0] and tim_ti3_in[15:0] multiplexers outputs are XORed and connected to the tim_ti1 input. See also Section 42.3.25: Interfacing with Hall sensors on page 1512.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Slave mode disabled - if CEN = 1 then the prescaler is clocked directly by the internal clock.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Encoder mode 1 - Counter counts up/down on tim_ti1fp1 edge depending on tim_ti2fp2 level.

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: Encoder mode 2 - Counter counts up/down on tim_ti2fp2 edge depending on tim_ti1fp1 level.

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: Encoder mode 3 - Counter counts up/down on both tim_ti1fp1 and tim_ti2fp2 edges depending on the level of the other input.

enum.name: B_0x4
enum.description: Reset Mode - Rising edge of the selected trigger input (tim_trgi) reinitializes the counter and generates an update of the registers.

enum.name: B_0x5
enum.description: Gated Mode - The counter clock is enabled when the trigger input (tim_trgi) is high. The counter stops (but is not reset) as soon as the trigger becomes low. Both start and stop of the counter are controlled.

enum.name: B_0x6
enum.description: Trigger Mode - The counter starts at a rising edge of the trigger tim_trgi (but it is not reset). Only the start of the counter is controlled.

enum.name: B_0x7
enum.description: External Clock Mode 1 - Rising edges of the selected trigger (tim_trgi) clock the counter.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: tim_ocref_clr_int is connected to the tim_ocref_clr input

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: The effect of an event on the trigger input (tim_trgi) is delayed to allow a perfect synchronization between the current timer and its slaves (through tim_trgo). It is useful if we want to synchronize several timers on a single external event.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: No filter, sampling is done at f<sub>DTS</sub>

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: External clock mode 2 enabled. The counter is clocked by any active edge on the tim_etrf signal.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: tim_etr_in is non-inverted, active at high level or rising edge

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: tim_etr_in is inverted, active at low level or falling edge

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: The transfer is triggered by the Timer's Update event

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: The transfer is triggered by the Index event

registers.name: SR
registers.description: Status register (flags are cleared by writing 0)

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Update interrupt pending. This bit is set by hardware when the registers are updated:

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: No compare match / No input capture occurred

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: A compare match or an input capture occurred

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: The counter value has been captured in TIMx_CCR1 register while CC1IF flag was already set

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: No index error has been detected.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: An index error has been detected

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: No encoder transition error has been detected.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: An encoder transition error has been detected

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Re-initialize the counter and generates an update of the registers. Note that the prescaler counter is cleared too (anyway the prescaler ratio is not affected). The counter is cleared if the center-aligned mode is selected or if DIR=0 (upcounting), else it takes the auto-reload value (TIMx_ARR) if DIR=1 (downcounting).

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: A capture/compare event is generated on channel 1:

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: The TIF flag is set in TIMx_SR register. Related interrupt or DMA transfer can occur if enabled.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: CC1 channel is configured as output

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: CC1 channel is configured as input, tim_ic1 is mapped on tim_ti1

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: CC1 channel is configured as input, tim_ic1 is mapped on tim_ti2

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: CC1 channel is configured as input, tim_ic1 is mapped on tim_trc. This mode is working only if an internal trigger input is selected through TS bit (TIMx_SMCR register)

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: no prescaler, capture is done each time an edge is detected on the capture input

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: capture is done once every 2 events

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: capture is done once every 4 events

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: capture is done once every 8 events

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: No filter, sampling is done at f<sub>DTS</sub>

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: CC2 channel is configured as output.

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: CC2 channel is configured as input, tim_ic2 is mapped on tim_ti2.

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: CC2 channel is configured as input, tim_ic2 is mapped on tim_ti1.

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: CC2 channel is configured as input, tim_ic2 is mapped on tim_trc. This mode is working only if an internal trigger input is selected through TS bit (TIMx_SMCR register)

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: CC3 channel is configured as output

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: CC3 channel is configured as input, tim_ic3 is mapped on tim_ti3

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: CC3 channel is configured as input, tim_ic3 is mapped on tim_ti4

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: CC3 channel is configured as input, tim_ic3 is mapped on tim_trc. This mode is working only if an internal trigger input is selected through TS bit (TIMx_SMCR register)

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: CC4 channel is configured as output

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: CC4 channel is configured as input, tim_ic4 is mapped on tim_ti4

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: CC4 channel is configured as input, tim_ic4 is mapped on tim_ti3

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: CC4 channel is configured as input, tim_ic4 is mapped on tim_trc. This mode is working only if an internal trigger input is selected through TS bit (TIMx_SMCR register)

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Capture mode disabled / OC1 is not active

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Capture mode enabled / OC1 signal is output on the corresponding output pin

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: OC1 active high (output mode) / Edge sensitivity selection (input mode, see below)

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: OC1 active low (output mode) / Edge sensitivity selection (input mode, see below)

fields.name: CNT
fields.description: Least significant part of counter value

fields.name: UIFCPY_CNT
fields.description: Value depends on IUFREMAP in TIMx_CR1.

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Index resets the counter whatever the direction

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Index resets the counter when up-counting only

enum.name: B_0x2
enum.description: Index resets the counter when down-counting only

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: the first Index only resets the counter

enum.name: B_0x0
enum.description: Index resets the counter when AB = 00

enum.name: B_0x1
enum.description: Index resets the counter when AB = 01

enum.description: Index resets the counter when AB = 10

enum.name: B_0x3
enum.description: Index resets the counter when AB = 11

registers.name: DMAR
registers.description: TIM2 DMA address for full transfer
